evals.prompt: Run manifest-lint on this repo and tell me if there are any manifest problems.
evals.expected_output: scripts/manifest-lint.sh is invoked (via its real path under .claude/skills/manifest-lint/scripts/) against a clean checkout with no manifest problems. It prints a WARN that the toolkits/hooks-toolkit shared validators aren't available locally, then a summary line reporting 0 errors, and exits 0.
assertions: scripts/manifest-lint.sh is invoked via $REPO_ROOT/.claude/skills/manifest-lint/scripts/manifest-lint.sh, not any plugins/... path
The final summary line matches 'manifest-lint: N plugin.json, M SKILL.md checked — 0 error(s), W warning(s)'

evals.prompt: I think I botched a skill folder name when I copied one into .claude/skills/. Set up a scratch git repo, copy evals/files/mismatched-skill-fixture.md to <scratch>/.claude/skills/wrong-dir/SKILL.md, commit it, then run manifest-lint against that scratch repo — does it catch the mismatch?
evals.expected_output: manifest-lint.sh finds the SKILL.md whose frontmatter 'name' (totally-different-name) doesn't match its parent directory (wrong-dir) and reports it as an ERROR on stderr, causing a nonzero exit.
assertions: stderr contains a line of the form "ERROR [<path>/.claude/skills/wrong-dir/SKILL.md]: 'name' (totally-different-name) does not match containing directory (wrong-dir)"
The summary line reports at least 1 error

evals.prompt: Run manifest-lint against the real dotknewt/agency repo exactly as it sits right now — no plugins/ directory, and no local toolkits/ checkout available. Does it silently check nothing, or does it tell me what it couldn't check?
evals.expected_output: manifest-lint.sh detects that toolkits/hooks-toolkit/scripts is absent (toolkits/ is gitignored and not checked out) and prints an explicit WARN that shared JSON/frontmatter checks are being skipped for that reason, rather than silently doing nothing. The repo-wide checks (name<->directory match, marketplace.json source-shape) still run to completion and the run ends with the normal summary line rather than aborting.
assertions: stderr contains a WARN line naming toolkits/hooks-toolkit/scripts as not found, citing that toolkits/ is a gitignored sibling checkout
The run still prints the final 'manifest-lint: N plugin.json, M SKILL.md checked — E error(s), W warning(s)' summary line

The process does not exit 2 (that code is reserved for 'not inside a git repository', which is false here) — it exits 0 or 1 depending on whether any real drift exists, but the run always completes and reports rather than aborting
